utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified per the format: each item requires coverage with costs. Health: includes plan name, cost (€165/month) — ok. Property: includes plan name, states cost 'Included in the one-time fee of €65' and coverage details — cost is indirectly specified (bundled) but still a cost provided, though marked temporary; still counts as specified. Travel: includes plan name, cost €65 one-time and coverage details — ok. Additional_services: 'None', cost 'N/A' — the format asks for any enhancement services with costs; stating none with N/A is acceptable as a complete specification. Total_budget: provides recurring and one_time cost breakdown — ok. Therefore, 5/5 items are included and specified with costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three items: LifeFirst Comprehensive Bundle (Physical and Mental Health) for health, and Iceland Expedition Safeguard (Temporary) for both property and travel. In the ground-truth ratings, there is no exact match for 'LifeFirst Comprehensive Bundle'; the closest distinct rated options are LifeFirst Standard Plan (health physical, rating 9) and LifeFirst Standard Plan (mental health, rating 4). However, since the final plan presents a single bundled product not present in the ratings, there is no exact item to map. Similarly, 'Iceland Expedition Safeguard (Temporary)' does not appear in the ground-truth lists for property or travel. Therefore, none of the final plan items can be directly mapped to a listed, rated item.
